Red Harbour Capital Holdings Limited — Operational Incident Note (Hong Kong), 02/06/2026, prepared by Group Compliance.
On 01/06/2026 a misconfigured permission on the “HR-Onboarding” share exposed select employee onboarding forms to a vendor user from Blue Banyan Services Ltd. for 47 minutes.
Affected data under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance included employee names, corporate emails, and probation letters; no full identification numbers were displayed, and salary fields were masked per our minimisation controls.
Separately, two investor KYC packets for Harbour Growth Fund I LP were stored in the same folder; these were accessed only by internal users, and a post-incident review confirmed no external download.
An automated backup briefly replicated the folder index to LotusEdge SG‑1 (Singapore) at 12:14 HKT; cross-border log export was authorised under Data‑Export Approval DTE‑2026‑044 for forensics.
DPA Schedule 2 (Sub‑processors) with Blue Banyan Services Ltd. and Schedule 4 (Cross‑Border Clauses) were in force at the time; the schedules list LotusEdge SG‑1 as an approved region.
No documents containing M&A term sheets or board papers were in the exposed share; inside‑information obligations under the Securities and Futures Ordinance were reviewed and a memo concluded no price‑sensitive information was disclosed.
A DSAR from an employee (Mr. Adrian Lau) received on 31/05/2026 was fulfilled on 02/06/2026; no DSAR remains outstanding, and an erasure request for two onboarding scans was completed with deletion hash RH‑DEL‑00931.
One investor in Harbour Growth Fund I LP withdrew consent for direct marketing on 30/05/2026; CRM entries were suppressed the same day and suppression confirmation was emailed to the investor.
Vendor storage now contains no live personal data following certified erasure on 02/06/2026 (Blue Banyan Certificate BB‑CE‑2026‑117); this is a completed control and does not indicate a continuing risk.
Retention periods for onboarding forms have been shortened from 24 to 18 months in the interim, and field‑level redaction has been applied to legacy scans to reinforce minimisation.
Policy note: we require encryption for all exports and prompt notification to authorities where thresholds are met; this sentence reflects policy only and is not a notification trigger.
Compliance Memo RH‑CPL‑2026‑M12 and the amended DPA schedules are attached; employment, fund, and M&A workstreams have acknowledged the corrective actions.
Contact: Ms. Yvonne Cho, Data Protection Officer, Red Harbour Capital Holdings Limited, dpo@redharbourcapital.hk; for deal‑team queries, write to compliance@redharbourcapital.hk.
